agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher contributions require higher fundraising expenditures (the proxy being professional fees + direct event expenses) to solicit and secure those donations.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Weisbrod (1998) shows that fundraising expense behaves as an investment to increase contributions, leading to a strong positive correlation.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant positive correlation was found. Nonprofits with higher total contributions have larger fundraising expenses, confirming the fundraising-as-investment model where solicitation efforts scale alongside donation revenues.
